item.title: 5/24: Sunday Morning
item.description: Hosted by Lee Cowan. Featured: Concours d'LeMons, Iranian propaganda, Patti LaBelle.

item.title: Shark kills spearfisher in front of friends: "Terrifying thing to see"
item.description: A spearfisher was killed on the Great Barrier Reef on Sunday in Australia's second fatal shark attack in just over a week, police said.

item.title: Efforts to stop California chemical leak in Orange County on "new trajectory," officials say
item.description: More than 50,000 people in California remain evacuated due to an Orange County chemical leak that prompted an emergency proclamation from Gov. Gavin Newsom.

item.title: Iran agrees in principle to dispose of highly-enriched uranium, official says
item.description: Negotiations are "in a very good place," a senior Trump official said, but, a deal to end the Iran war likely will not be signed this weekend.

item.title: Transcript: Lt. Col. William Swenson (Ret.) and Command Sergeant Major Matthew Williams (Ret.) on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," May 24, 2026
item.description: The following is the transcript of the interview with retired Lt. Col. William Swenson and retired Command Sergeant Major Matthew Williams (Ret.) that aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on May 24, 2026.

item.title: Transcript: Dr. Deborah Birx on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," May 24, 2026
item.description: The following is the transcript of the interview with Dr. Deborah Birx that aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on May 24, 2026.

item.title: Transcript: Kevin Hassett on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," May 24, 2026
item.description: The following is the transcript of the interview with Kevin Hassett, director of the White House National Economic Council, that aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on May 24, 2026.

item.title: Transcript: Sen. Chris Van Hollen on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," May 24, 2026
item.description: The following is the transcript of the interview with Sen. Chris Van Hollen, Democrat of Maryland, that aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on May 24, 2026.

item.title: Patti LaBelle on being called a legend: "I have earned it"
item.description: Patti LaBelle said she's "having fun living it down" as the legendary singer marks her 82nd birthday.

item.title: Dr. Deborah Birx says "there's a deep bench" to CDC, FDA to respond to Ebola
item.description: Dr. Deborah Birx, who helped coordinate the international response to the 2014 Ebola outbreak, told "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" that "there's a deep bench" at the CDC, FDA to facilitate an Ebola response despite that neither agency has a confirmed agency head.

item.title: Kevin Hassett says "consumer sentiment" should be renamed "political sentiment"
item.description: As the Dow Jones hit an all-time high this week, consumer sentiment hit an all-time low, Kevin Hassett, the director of the White House National Economic Council, told "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" that he believes "we should stop calling it consumer sentiment and start calling it political sentiment, because it's really a political variable."

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (May 24)
item.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Lee Cowan.

item.title: Transcript: Reps. Josh Gottheimer and Mike Lawler on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," May 24, 2026
item.description: The following is the transcript of the interview with Reps. Josh Gottheimer, Democrat of New Jersey, and Mike Lawler, Republican of New York, that aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on May 24, 2026.

item.title: China launches spacecraft with astronaut set to remain in space for a year
item.description: China has launched the Shenzhou 23 spacecraft with three astronauts heading to its space station.

item.title: The legendary Patti LaBelle
item.description: Legendary singer Patti LaBelle has been performing for six decades. Correspondent Tracy Smith talks with LaBelle at her hometown of Philadelphia as she marks her 82nd birthday.

item.title: 5/24: Face The Nation
item.description: This week on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," as President Trump says a peace deal with Iran has been "largely negotiated," Imtiaz Tyab reports from Tel Aviv and Sen. Chris Van Hollen discusses the possible deal. Plus, on this Memorial Day weekend, Medal of Honor recipients retired Lt. Col. William Swenson and retired Command Sergeant Major Matthew Williams join.

item.title: Rep. Mike Lawler: "Shameful" that "people feel it's okay to engage" in antisemitic conduct
item.description: Reps. Mike Lawler and Josh Gottheimer, who are on the House Problem Solvers Caucus, have both been working to tide the rise of antisemitism in the U.S. Lawler, who recently was subjected to an antisemitic comment by Sen. Rand Paul's son, told "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" that "the idea that people feel it's okay to engage in that type of conduct is shameful."

item.title: Sen. Chris Van Hollen says "we should get rid of this political slush fund" about DOJ fund
item.description: Democratic Sen. Chris Van Hollen told "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" that he hopes that there is an appetite within the Republican conference to "get rid of this political slush fund," referring to the Justice Department's anti-weaponization fund. "This is really corrupt from the start," he added.

item.title: U.S., Iran have agreed to broad principles of deal, Trump official says
item.description: There is broad commitment on the principles of a deal, a senior Trump administration official said, and the administration feels positive about where things stand.

item.title: Medal of Honor recipients Lt. Col. William Swenson (Ret.), Command Sgt. Matthew Williams (Ret.)
item.description: On Memorial Day weekend, as America plans to celebrate its 250th birthday, Margaret Brennan sat down with two Medal of Honor recipients, retired Army Lt. Col. William Swenson and retired Army Command Sergeant Major Matthew Williams, both of whom were awarded their medals for valor in battle during their service in Afghanistan.

item.title: Video shows infant being rescued from car trapped in Texas floodwaters
item.description: First responders rescued an infant from a car that became trapped in floodwaters in southern Texas on Saturday, video shows.

item.title: Face the Nation: Hassett, Birx, Swenson and Williams
item.description: Missed the second half of the show? White House National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett, Dr. Deborah Birx and Medal of Honor recipients retired Army Lt. Col. William Swenson and retired Army Command Sergeant Major Matthew Williams.

item.title: Inside Iran's online propaganda war
item.description: An online propaganda war is currently being waged between the United States and Iran, and global reaction suggests the Iranians are beating the Americans at their own game.

item.title: Inside Iran's online propaganda war
item.description: As the war with Iran approaches the three-month mark, another battle is raging far from the front lines. Ted Koppel examines how Iran is using AI, satire and social media in the online propaganda war. In a conflict where clicks can matter more than facts, the most powerful weapon may be the message that lingers after the scrolling stops.

item.title: John McWhorter on being "strange," Fats Waller's lost Broadway musical
item.description: John McWhorter talks about his book, "Losing the Race: Self-Sabotage in Black America," published in 2000, along with one of his latest projects.

item.title: John McWhorter on controversy and reconstructing Fats Waller's "Early to Bed"
item.description: New York Times columnist, bestselling author, linguist and Columbia University professor John McWhorter doesn't shy away from controversy. He talks with Mo Rocca about his book, "Losing the Race: Self-Sabatoge in Black America," published in 2000, along with one of his latest projects, reconstructing the long-forgotten 1940s hit Broadway musical "Early to Bed" with music by jazz legend Fats Waller, and why he feels good about how people perceive him now.

item.title: Recipe: Lavender and lemon poppy seed cake from "Cake Picnic"
item.description: Elisa Sunga shares how to make lavender and lemon poppy seed cake in her cookbook, "Cake Picnic: Recipes for the Love of Cake & Friends."

item.title: A cake picnic worth cutting into
item.description: For the last two years, thousands of dessert lovers have gathered at a Cake Picnic, hosted in cities around the world, to show off their showstopping baked creations or their store-bought favorites. The rule: no cake, no entry. Founder Elisa Sunga shares with Faith Salie how she baked up the recipe for the touring Cake Picnic.

item.title: 2 dead, dozens wounded after Russia launches large attack on Ukraine
item.description: Russia used a powerful hypersonic missile that has nuclear capabilities to carry out a massive attack Sunday in Kyiv, Ukraine.

item.title: Trump administration to require most green card applicants to leave U.S. first
item.description: The Trump administration on Friday announced a sweeping policy designed to make it harder for immigrants already in the U.S. to get permanent residency.
